title: Summary of key monetary policy variables
summary: The Bank of Canada's key monetary policy variables table shows the overnight rate target held at 2.25% as of February 2026, with total CPI at 1.8% (within the 1-3% inflation-control target band). Core inflation measures (CPI-trim at 2.3%, CPI-median at 2.3%, CPI-common at 2.4%) remain anchored near the midpoint of the target.
key_points: Overnight rate target remained at 2.25% in February 2026, steady since September 2025 when it was cut from 2.50%
Total CPI was 1.8% in February 2026, within the Bank of Canada's 1-3% inflation-control target range
Core inflation measures: CPI-trim 2.3%, CPI-median 2.3%, CPI-common 2.4% (12-month growth rates)
The Bank of Canada rate peaked at 5.00% between February 2024 and April 2024, before entering an easing cycle
Yield spread between conventional and Real Return bonds was 2.03% in February 2026
Monetary aggregates show M1+ at 7.2% growth and M1++ at 7.0% growth in January 2026
why_it_matters: This Bank of Canada data dashboard provides the official monthly record of Canadian monetary policy settings and inflation trajectory. The overnight rate at 2.25% signals the current policy stance during an easing cycle from the 5.00% peak reached in early 2024, with inflation sitting at the lower end of the target band. This data is essential reference for tracking the evolution of Canadian monetary conditions.
macro_context: Canada's overnight rate has declined from the 5.00% peak (Feb-Apr 2024) to the current 2.25% level as of February 2026. The rate was cut from 2.50% to 2.25% in October 2025 and has remained unchanged since. Total CPI at 1.8% is at the lower end of the 1-3% target band, while core measures cluster around 2.3-2.4%, suggesting the Bank of Canada has successfully anchored inflation near target. The Canadian Effective Exchange Rate (CEER) was 113.76 in February 2026.

issues_found: Minor timing discrepancy: Record states overnight rate was 'steady since September 2025 when it was cut from 2.50%', but source data shows rate changed from 2.50% in September 2025 to 2.25% in October 2025 — the cut occurred in October, not September

Summary and why_it_matters are thin: The record accurately reflects the data but lacks interpretation or analytical value beyond presenting numbers. For a data dashboard with no narrative content, the summary could be enhanced to better contextualize the significance
Source type 'data_dashboard' is appropriate but borderline on archival value threshold — it's purely tabular data without any commentary or analysis

human_review.notes: Issues are minor and factual. The record is mostly accurate with one small timing discrepancy in key_points. Source attribution is correct, event_type is reasonable, and key numbers are accurate. A human reviewer could quickly confirm the October 2025 rate cut timing and decide whether the summary adds sufficient value for archival purposes.

notes: This is an official Bank of Canada statistical dashboard providing monthly time-series data for Canadian monetary policy variables. The source is authoritative and high-quality but contains no narrative analysis or policy commentary—only raw/tabular data. The most recent data point is February 2026, making this essentially current. Relevant to macro catalysts as it tracks the evolution of Bank of Canada rate decisions and inflation metrics over multiple years.
